summary: PR #2433 decompiled the Snap menu per-frame update function fn_802545C4 in src/melee/mn/mnsnap.c as a large state-machine switch for slot selection, photo browsing, dialogs, and copy/move/delete card operations. It also updated mnsnap.h prototypes for mnSnap_8025409C and mnSnap_8025441C. Review focused on reverting expanded assert/string storage hacks and keeping HSD_ASSERT and inline format strings instead.

key_files.role: Main decompilation target; adds/reworks fn_802545C4, dialog text helper, prototype-driven callsite changes, and matching tactics such as PAD_STACK and a dont_inline pragma.
evidence_refs: pr_body:decompile main per-frame update for Snap menu; function is 99.373% match
diff:fn_802545C4 large switch cases 0-23 and mnSnap_InitDialogText

key_files.role: Header prototype cleanup for recovered function signatures.
evidence_refs: pr_body:fixes mnSnap_8025409C arg type and mnSnap_8025441C return type
diff:mnSnap_8025409C changed from HSD_JObj* jobj_flag to s32 dlg_type

what_changed.text: Decompiled fn_802545C4, the Snap menu main per-frame update, as a state-machine switch covering states 0-23 for slot selection, photo browsing, dialog confirmation, and copy/move/delete card operations.
Changed button input handling in fn_802545C4 to use u64 buttons and assigns mn_804A04F0.buttons from mn_80229624(4).
Added a static inline helper mnSnap_InitDialogText for repeated dialog text allocation/setup using HSD_SisLib_803A5ACC and HSD_Text font/default fields.
Updated mnSnap_8025409C from a pointer-like parameter to s32 dlg_type and adjusted comparisons/calls to use integer dialog type values.
Updated mnSnap_8025441C return type according to the PR body; exact header diff details are not loaded beyond changed-file metadata and PR text.
The bot report recorded one unmatched-item improvement for main/melee/mn/mnsnap fn_802545C4 from 80.82% to 98.94%, while the PR body/title state 99.37%/99.373%.

smart_moves.text: Factored repeated dialog text initialization into mnSnap_InitDialogText, reducing duplicated setup of dlg_text, alignment, kerning, and font size across state transitions.
Recovered mnSnap_8025409C's parameter as a dialog type integer instead of treating it as an HSD_JObj pointer cast, improving signature truthfulness and callsite readability.
Kept ordinary loops for repeated thumbnail/count label formatting in mnSnap_80253964 rather than expanding per-slot code.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Expanded assert blocks that only spell out __assert calls should be reverted to HSD_ASSERT when the original macro form and implicit strings are available.

Do not introduce named SDATA/static string storage for ordinary assert strings or format literals merely because generated output exposes those addresses; keep literals inline unless data ownership evidence justifies the storage.
When a recovered function body proves a more accurate signature, update the owning header and callsites rather than preserving fake pointer casts.
evidence_refs: diff:mnSnap_8025409C HSD_JObj* jobj_flag to s32 dlg_type

naming_conventions.text: The PR uses descriptive local names for recovered state-machine concepts such as dlg_type, active_slot, other_slot, dlg_timer, dlg_active, dlg_text, slot_a_jobj, slot_b_jobj, and fullview_jobj where roles are evident from mnsnap state fields.
No evidence supports accepting new semantic global names for the attempted assert/format strings; reviewer questioned the need and author said the hack was reverted.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The diff shows the tactic, but loaded evidence does not include objdiff rationale beyond the PR's percentage claim and bot report.
The pragma is tightly scoped in the diff; loaded evidence does not show reviewer discussion or adjacent-function verification for the pragma.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Introduced a small static inline helper for repeated dialog text initialization, likely to help source shape while avoiding large duplicated blocks.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Evidence supports the helper's presence and repeated use; exact matching rationale is not documented in loaded comments.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used direct global mnSnap_804A0A10 field accesses in fn_802545C4 rather than preserving a local snap pointer throughout.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The diff shows the access pattern, but loaded evidence does not explain whether this was required for matching.

review_feedback.comment: Expanded __assert blocks should be reverted; use HSD_ASSERT with implicit strings.
Format strings such as "%03d" and "%d" were likely inline constants rather than variables that were inlined.
Questioned the need for added SDATA/static string declarations and the local inline block in the shown hunk.
Author replied that the questioned string/assert hack was reverted as unnecessary.

follow_up_queries: mnsnap SDATA jobj jobj.h reverted unnecessary hack

notes: PR identity, changed files, title/body, review comments, and changed-file counts are well supported.
The raw diff is truncated, so details from later fn_802545C4 switch cases and exact mnsnap.h header hunk are not fully visible in loaded evidence.
The PR title/body claim 99.37%/99.373%, while the bot report in text_corpus shows fn_802545C4 improving to 98.94%; this postmortem records both without resolving the discrepancy.
Review comments show requested cleanup and author response for the string/assert hack, but loaded evidence does not include a final full diff proving every requested change after revision.
Matching tactics such as PAD_STACK and #pragma dont_inline are visible in the diff but lack loaded objdiff/adjacent-regression rationale.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use HSD_ASSERT instead of expanded __assert blocks when implicit assert strings match
accepted_candidate_records.text: In mnsnap review, expanded __assert sequences for jobj/dobj checks were called out for revert in favor of HSD_ASSERT with implicit strings. This reinforces the existing assert macro cleanup rule.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep ordinary format strings inline unless data ownership is proven
accepted_candidate_records.text: Reviewer feedback on mnsnap noted that "%03d" and related format strings were likely inline constants, not variables that were inlined. Added SDATA/static string declarations were questioned and the author said the hack was reverted.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recovered signatures should flow into headers and callsites
accepted_candidate_records.text: The PR body says mnsnap.h prototypes were fixed for mnSnap_8025409C and mnSnap_8025441C; the diff shows mnSnap_8025409C's parameter becoming s32 dlg_type and callsites using integer dialog types.

source_update_candidates.title: Add mnsnap as an example for inline literals over generated SDATA strings
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2433 review provides a concrete example where generated-looking SDATA/static strings for "jobj", "jobj.h", "%03d", and "%d" were questioned, and the author described the change as a reverted unnecessary hack. This could be cited under literals-and-data-ownership or no-string-literal-symbol-regression if examples are desired.
source_update_candidates.reason: Existing standards already cover the rule; this PR offers a grounded example for future curation.

source_update_candidates.title: Add mnsnap as an example for preferring HSD_ASSERT over expanded __assert
source_update_candidates.text: Reviewer feedback explicitly requested reverting expanded __assert blocks and using HSD_ASSERT with implicit strings in mnsnap. This can be added as an example under assert-report-macros.
source_update_candidates.reason: The global standard already exists; curator may add this PR as supporting historical evidence.

rejection_notes: Do not treat the attempted SDATA/static declarations for assert and format strings as accepted path facts; review evidence says they were questioned and author called them an unnecessary hack.

Do not infer that PAD_STACK(336), direct global field access, or #pragma dont_inline are generally accepted for mnsnap; loaded evidence shows use but not the objdiff rationale or adjacent regression checks.
Do not claim final exact match status beyond the PR title/body and bot report values; loaded evidence contains conflicting percentages and no final objdiff output.
